diagrams.title: 2×2 Complete Board
diagrams.description: A 2×2 chessboard with all 4 squares available for rook placement
elements: r_2 = 2 ways to place 2 non-attacking rooks: (1,1)-(2,2) and (1,2)-(2,1)
diagrams.purpose: Understanding basic rook polynomial computation and non-attacking placements

diagrams.title: Disjoint Boards Example
diagrams.description: Two separate 2×2 boards with no shared rows or columns
elements: No overlap in rows or columns between boards
Product formula applies: R = R₁ × R₂
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing when to apply product formula for disjoint boards

diagrams.title: Non-attacking vs Attacking Rooks
diagrams.description: Comparison of valid (non-attacking) and invalid (attacking) rook placements
elements: Valid: rooks in different rows AND different columns
Invalid: rooks sharing same row OR same column
Shows why diagonal placements are valid but same row/column are not
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the fundamental condition for non-attacking placements

diagrams.title: 3×3 Rook Polynomial Coefficients
diagrams.description: Bar chart showing the coefficients in R_3 = 1 + 9x + 18x² + 6x³
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how coefficients change and verifying calculation
